socialClasses.en: Major social classes included state bureaucratic and security elites, oligarchs and large business owners, middle-class professionals, workers, rural inhabitants, pensioners, unemployed and underemployed people, and minority communities across republics and frontier regions

allies.zh: 俄罗斯在2000年没有固定意义上的正式军事盟友集团，但与白俄罗斯保持联盟国家框架，与亚美尼亚、印度、塞尔维亚传统友好，并通过独联体维持对前苏联空间的影响
allies.en: Russia in 2000 had no fixed alliance bloc in the strict sense, but maintained a Union State framework with Belarus, traditional friendly ties with Armenia, India, and Serbia, and sought influence across the former Soviet space through the CIS

enemies.zh: 无正式宣战敌国；实际对立和安全摩擦对象包括车臣武装与北高加索叛乱网络，以及在战略层面引发警惕的北约扩张趋势和部分西方干预政策
enemies.en: It had no formally declared enemy state; in practice, opposing forces included Chechen militants and insurgent networks in the North Caucasus, as well as NATO enlargement and certain Western interventionist policies that raised strategic alarm

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策强调恢复大国地位、维护联合国安理会常任理事国影响力、反对单极霸权、保持独联体主导地位、发展对华与对印关系，并在欧洲与美国之间寻求现实主义平衡
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized restoring great-power status, preserving influence as a permanent member of the UN Security Council, opposing unipolar hegemony, maintaining primacy in the CIS, developing ties with China and India, and seeking a realist balance with Europe and the United States

keyInnovations.zh: 俄罗斯继承苏联在航天、核能、导弹、数学、物理学、军工航空和重工业设计方面的深厚基础；2000年在信息技术和消费电子方面相对落后，但在火箭发动机、核技术、防空系统和基础科学上仍具国际竞争力
keyInnovations.en: Russia inherited deep Soviet strengths in space, nuclear energy, missiles, mathematics, physics, military aviation, and heavy industrial design; in 2000 it lagged in information technology and consumer electronics, but remained internationally competitive in rocket engines, nuclear technology, air defense systems, and basic science

strengths.zh: 幅员辽阔、资源极其丰富、核威慑强大、教育和科研基础深厚、安理会常任理事国地位稳固，并且在欧亚地缘政治上拥有巨大纵深
strengths.en: Its strengths were vast territory, immense natural resources, a powerful nuclear deterrent, deep educational and scientific foundations, a secure UN Security Council seat, and enormous geopolitical depth across Eurasia

weaknesses.zh: 经济结构过度依赖能源与原材料、法治和产权环境薄弱、腐败严重、人口与健康危机突出、地方治理不均、常规军队现代化不足且金融体系脆弱
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included heavy dependence on energy and raw materials, weak rule of law and property-rights protections, serious corruption, acute demographic and health crises, uneven regional governance, inadequate conventional military modernization, and a fragile financial system

outlook.zh: 2000年的俄罗斯总体稳定并处于国家能力重建初期；若高油价持续、中央集权加强且财政改革成功，国家实力有望恢复，但长期前景将受制度质量、经济多元化和人口趋势制约
outlook.en: Russia in 2000 was broadly stable and at an early stage of rebuilding state capacity; if high oil prices persisted, central authority strengthened, and fiscal reforms succeeded, national power was likely to recover, but the long-term outlook would be constrained by institutional quality, economic diversification, and demographic trends

description.zh: 后苏联大国，于2000年进入普京时代，横跨欧亚。
description.en: A major post-Soviet power entering the Putin era in 2000 and spanning Europe and Asia.

name.zh: 中亚后苏联共和国联合体（哈萨克斯坦、乌兹别克斯坦、土库曼斯坦、吉尔吉斯斯坦、塔吉克斯坦）
name.en: Confederated Post-Soviet Central Asian Republics (Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan)

ruler.zh: 努尔苏丹·纳扎尔巴耶夫、伊斯兰·卡里莫夫、萨帕尔穆拉特·尼亚佐夫、阿斯卡尔·阿卡耶夫、埃莫马利·拉赫莫诺夫等诸总统
ruler.en: Various presidents including Nursultan Nazarbayev, Islam Karimov, Saparmurat Niyazov, Askar Akayev, and Emomali Rakhmonov

capital.zh: 阿斯塔纳、塔什干、阿什哈巴德、比什凯克、杜尚别
capital.en: Astana, Tashkent, Ashgabat, Bishkek, and Dushanbe

governmentForm.zh: 以主权共和国为基础的松散地区性联合体，各成员国维持本国总统制政体并在安全、贸易与交通事务上协调合作
governmentForm.en: A loose regional confederative grouping of sovereign republics, with each member retaining its own presidential system while coordinating on security, trade, and transport affairs

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构由总统及其核心精英、国家官僚体系、安全机构、城市专业阶层、国有企业与新兴商人、集体农庄改组后的农村居民、游牧与半游牧传统社群及劳工群体构成，俄语城市人口与主体民族人口并存
socialStructure.en: The social structure consisted of presidents and core elites, state bureaucracies, security organs, urban professional classes, state-enterprise managers and emerging businessmen, rural populations reorganized from collective farms, traditional nomadic and semi-nomadic communities, and laboring groups, with Russian-speaking urban populations coexisting alongside titular national majorities

rulingClass.zh: 执政阶层主要为各共和国总统、总统府幕僚、安全机构高层、地区行政长官、国有能源与工业企业管理者以及与国家关系密切的商业寡头
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed primarily of the presidents of the republics, presidential administrations, senior security officials, regional governors, managers of state energy and industrial enterprises, and politically connected business oligarchs

succession.zh: 名义上通过宪法规定的总统选举和议会程序实现，但实际继承高度依赖执政精英网络、国家机器控制与安全机构支持
succession.en: Succession was nominally determined through constitutional presidential elections and parliamentary procedures, but in practice depended heavily on ruling elite networks, control of state institutions, and support from security services

structure.zh: 该联合体并非统一中央国家，而是由五个独立共和国组成的协调体系；各国普遍实行强总统制，总统府凌驾于议会与内阁之上，地方由州、州级市、省、区和县级行政单位管理，跨国合作主要通过首脑会晤、双边协议及独联体等框架进行
structure.en: This confederation was not a unitary central state but a coordinating system of five independent republics; each generally operated a strong presidential regime in which the presidential apparatus outweighed parliament and cabinet, while local administration was conducted through provinces, regions, and districts, and interstate cooperation proceeded mainly through summit meetings, bilateral agreements, and CIS-related frameworks

name.zh: 总统府与国家办公厅体系

function.zh: 负责总统决策协调、人事任命、政策监督、国内安全指导和跨部门统筹，是各共和国实际权力核心
function.en: Responsible for coordinating presidential decision-making, appointments, policy oversight, domestic security direction, and cross-ministerial management; they formed the practical core of power in each republic

function.zh: 管理财政、工业、能源、农业、交通、贸易和私有化政策，维持从苏联计划体系向市场经济转型的国家调控
function.en: Managed finance, industry, energy, agriculture, transport, trade, and privatization policy, sustaining state regulation during the transition from Soviet planning to market economies

function.zh: 负责警务、情报、反对派监控、反极端主义、边境管制和政权安全，尤其在乌兹别克斯坦、土库曼斯坦和塔吉克斯坦影响显著
function.en: Handled policing, intelligence, opposition surveillance, anti-extremism, border control, and regime security, with especially strong influence in Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan, and Tajikistan

name.en: Foreign Affairs and Interstate Cooperation Ministries

function.zh: 管理与俄罗斯、中国、美国、土耳其、伊朗及国际金融机构的关系，并推动能源出口、地区通道与安全合作
function.en: Managed relations with Russia, China, the United States, Turkey, Iran, and international financial institutions, while promoting energy exports, regional transit corridors, and security cooperation

name.zh: 司法、检察与立法支持机构
name.en: Justice, Procuracy, and Legislative Support Bodies

function.zh: 负责法院行政、检察监督、法典修订、宪法解释与议会程序支持，但通常受行政权强烈影响
function.en: Oversaw court administration, procuratorial supervision, legal code revision, constitutional interpretation, and legislative support, though usually under strong executive influence

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由州、省、区、县和市级政府构成，地方首长多由总统任命或受中央强力影响；传统氏族、地区网络与地方精英在资源分配和社会控制中仍具重要作用
localAdmin.en: Local administration consisted of provincial, regional, district, and municipal governments, with many local heads appointed by presidents or heavily influenced by central authority; traditional clan, regional, and local elite networks remained important in resource distribution and social control

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以苏联大陆法传统为基础，1990年代后引入新宪法、民商法和投资法；法院形式上独立，但实际常受行政机关、检察系统和安全机构影响，伊斯兰法主要存在于社会习俗层面而非国家法源
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on the Soviet civil-law tradition, with new constitutions, civil-commercial codes, and investment laws introduced after the 1990s; courts were formally independent but in practice often influenced by executive authorities, procuracies, and security organs, while Islamic law persisted mostly at the level of social custom rather than state jurisprudence

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括企业利润税、增值税、关税、消费税、个人所得税、土地税和自然资源开采相关收入；资源型国家尤其依赖石油、天然气和矿产出口收益，较贫困山地国家则更多依赖间接税、外援和汇款带动的消费税基
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included enterprise profit taxes, value-added taxes, customs duties, excises, personal income taxes, land taxes, and revenues tied to resource extraction; resource-rich states relied especially on oil, gas, and mineral export receipts, while poorer mountainous states depended more on indirect taxes, foreign aid, and remittance-driven consumption bases

religion.zh: 主要宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教，辅以什叶派少数群体、俄罗斯东正教徒及少量其他宗教社群；在国家层面普遍采取世俗主义，但宗教复兴在社会生活中逐渐增强
religion.en: The principal religion was Sunni Islam, alongside smaller Shi'a communities, Russian Orthodox believers, and minor religious groups; at the state level the region generally maintained secularism, but religious revival was increasingly visible in social life

philosophy.zh: 主导思想为国家世俗主义、后苏联民族建构、温和现代化、主权优先与对传统突厥—波斯—游牧文化遗产的重新诠释，同时保留苏联时期教育理性主义和技术官僚倾向
philosophy.en: The dominant outlook combined state secularism, post-Soviet nation-building, moderate modernization, sovereignty-first politics, and renewed interpretation of Turkic, Persian, and nomadic cultural legacies, while retaining Soviet-era educational rationalism and technocratic tendencies

writingSystem.zh: 书写体系呈多样化：俄语和塔吉克语主要使用西里尔字母，哈萨克语、吉尔吉斯语和乌兹别克语在西里尔与拉丁化政策之间并存过渡，土库曼语则更积极推进拉丁字母改革
writingSystem.en: Writing systems were diverse: Russian and Tajik largely used Cyrillic, while Kazakh, Kyrgyz, and Uzbek operated in varying transitional combinations of Cyrillic usage and Latinization policy, and Turkmen pursued Latin-script reform more aggressively

culturalAchievements.zh: 该地区在2000年前后延续丝绸之路城市文明、草原史诗、波斯—突厥文学、苏联教育与艺术机构遗产，在建筑修复、民族史书写、传统音乐舞蹈、地毯工艺、骑马文化与伊斯兰古城保护方面具有显著成就；撒马尔罕、布哈拉、希瓦和突厥斯坦等历史中心继续象征地区文明连续性
culturalAchievements.en: Around 2000 the region sustained the legacy of Silk Road urban civilization, steppe epic traditions, Persian-Turkic literature, and Soviet educational and artistic institutions, showing notable achievement in architectural restoration, national historiography, traditional music and dance, carpet craftsmanship, equestrian culture, and the preservation of Islamic old cities; historic centers such as Samarkand, Bukhara, Khiva, and Turkistan continued to symbolize the region's civilizational continuity

languageFamily.zh: 语言以突厥语族为主，包括哈萨克语、乌兹别克语、土库曼语和吉尔吉斯语；塔吉克语属伊朗语族，俄语则继续作为跨共和国交流、行政与高等教育的重要通用语
languageFamily.en: Languages were predominantly Turkic, including Kazakh, Uzbek, Turkmen, and Kyrgyz; Tajik belonged to the Iranian branch, while Russian continued to serve as an important lingua franca for inter-republic communication, administration, and higher education

gdpDescription.zh: 该联合体经济处于后苏联转型阶段，内部差异显著：哈萨克斯坦与土库曼斯坦依赖能源出口，乌兹别克斯坦兼具棉花、黄金与较完整工业基础，吉尔吉斯斯坦和塔吉克斯坦则相对贫弱，依赖农业、水电、黄金、外援和劳动力流动。总体上市场化推进不均，国家干预程度较高，基础设施承袭苏联体系
gdpDescription.en: The confederation's economy was in a post-Soviet transitional stage with sharp internal variation: Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan depended on energy exports, Uzbekistan combined cotton, gold, and a relatively broad industrial base, while Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan were comparatively poor and relied on agriculture, hydropower, gold, foreign aid, and labor mobility. Overall, marketization was uneven, state intervention remained significant, and infrastructure largely inherited the Soviet system

mainIndustries.zh: 石油、天然气、棉花种植与加工、黄金和有色金属开采、铀矿、机械制造、化肥、纺织、食品加工、水电、交通运输与边境贸易
mainIndustries.en: Oil, natural gas, cotton cultivation and processing, gold and nonferrous metal mining, uranium, machinery, fertilizers, textiles, food processing, hydropower, transportation, and border trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以原油、天然气、棉花、黄金、铀、铜、锌、铝、电力和化工产品为主；进口以机械设备、车辆、消费品、药品、电子产品和部分粮食为主
tradeGoods.en: Exports were led by crude oil, natural gas, cotton, gold, uranium, copper, zinc, aluminum, electricity, and chemical products; imports focused on machinery, vehicles, consumer goods, pharmaceuticals, electronics, and some grain

name.zh: 坚戈、苏姆、马纳特、索姆、索莫尼与塔吉克卢布并存
name.en: Tenge, Som, Manat, Som, and Somoni/Tajik Ruble in parallel usage

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分布极不均衡，城市行政与能源部门家庭拥有较高现金收入和耐用品，农村家庭更多依赖自给农业、牲畜、小规模市场交易和亲属网络；通胀冲击后的储蓄恢复仍然有限
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal, with urban administrative and energy-sector households enjoying higher cash incomes and durable goods, while rural families relied more on subsistence farming, livestock, small-scale market exchange, and kinship networks; savings recovery after inflationary shocks remained limited

economicSystem.zh: 混合型后苏联转型经济，结合私有化、国家控制关键资源、行政定价残余、寡头资本、国有企业和外资能源开发；不同共和国在开放程度上差异明显
economicSystem.en: A mixed post-Soviet transitional economy combining privatization, state control of strategic resources, residual administrative pricing, oligarchic capital, state enterprises, and foreign-backed energy development; openness varied substantially among the republics

source.zh: 外援、国企分红与其他收入
source.en: Foreign Aid, State Enterprise Transfers, and Other Income

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备在各国之间极不均衡，哈萨克斯坦和土库曼斯坦拥有较强外汇与资源收益支撑，乌兹别克斯坦维持较高国家控制下的财政动员能力，吉尔吉斯斯坦和塔吉克斯坦的现金储备则较为脆弱
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury strength was highly uneven across the republics: Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan enjoyed stronger foreign-exchange and resource-backed positions, Uzbekistan maintained relatively high fiscal mobilization under state control, while Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan possessed more fragile cash reserves

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体偏稳定导向，强调维持国家工资、补贴和安全支出，同时通过资源出口、国际贷款和有限税改缓解转型压力；部分国家采取强行政控制与多重汇率管理以稳定物价和外汇
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly stability-oriented, emphasizing maintenance of state wages, subsidies, and security expenditure while easing transition pressures through resource exports, international borrowing, and limited tax reform; some states used strong administrative controls and multiple exchange-rate arrangements to stabilize prices and foreign exchange

description.zh: 以摩托化步兵、装甲部队、炮兵和山地部队为核心，装备多继承自苏联，承担边境防御、内部稳定和反叛乱任务
description.en: Centered on motorized infantry, armor, artillery, and mountain units, largely equipped with inherited Soviet matériel, and tasked with border defense, internal stability, and counterinsurgency

description.zh: 拥有有限数量的战斗机、运输机、直升机和地空导弹系统，主要用于领空巡逻、运输支援和防空值班
description.en: Maintained limited numbers of fighters, transports, helicopters, and surface-to-air missile systems, used mainly for airspace patrol, transport support, and air-defense alert duties

description.zh: 负责漫长边界线、毒品走私通道、阿富汗方向渗透风险和国内秩序维护，在塔吉克斯坦和乌兹别克斯坦尤其重要
description.en: Responsible for long borders, narcotics trafficking routes, infiltration risks from the Afghan direction, and domestic order, with particular importance in Tajikistan and Uzbekistan

name.zh: 国民警卫队、总统卫队与特种部队
name.en: National Guards, Presidential Guards, and Special Forces

description.zh: 承担政权保卫、反恐、关键设施保护和快速反应任务，常直接受总统或安全机构控制
description.en: Handled regime protection, counterterrorism, strategic facility security, and rapid response missions, often under direct presidential or security-service control

commanderInChief.zh: 各成员国总统为本国武装力量最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The president of each member republic served as commander-in-chief of its national armed forces

technology.zh: 军事技术以苏联遗产为主，包括T-72坦克、BMP步兵战车、米格和苏霍伊系列飞机、米系列直升机、S-75/S-125/S-200及部分较新型防空系统；现代化水平有限，维护、训练与后勤质量参差不齐
technology.en: Military technology was based primarily on Soviet inheritance, including T-72 tanks, BMP infantry fighting vehicles, MiG and Sukhoi aircraft, Mil helicopters, and S-75/S-125/S-200 with some newer air-defense systems; modernization was limited, and maintenance, training, and logistics quality were uneven

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括阿富汗方向的不稳定外溢、伊斯兰运动和武装渗透、边境划界争端、毒品走私、族群紧张、费尔干纳盆地冲突风险，以及大国在能源与安全事务中的竞争影响
threats.en: Main threats included spillover instability from Afghanistan, Islamist movements and armed infiltration, border-demarcation disputes, narcotics trafficking, ethnic tensions, conflict risks in the Ferghana Valley, and the impact of great-power competition in energy and security affairs

populationDescription.zh: 人口约五千六百万，分布极不均衡：乌兹别克斯坦人口最为稠密，哈萨克斯坦领土最大但密度较低，塔吉克斯坦与吉尔吉斯斯坦多山，土库曼斯坦人口较少且多集中于绿洲与城市带。族群上包括哈萨克人、乌兹别克人、土库曼人、吉尔吉斯人、塔吉克人、俄罗斯人、乌克兰人、德意志裔、鞑靼人、维吾尔人及其他少数民族
populationDescription.en: The population of about 56 million was very unevenly distributed: Uzbekistan was the most densely populated, Kazakhstan had the largest territory but low density, Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan were heavily mountainous, and Turkmenistan had a smaller population concentrated in oases and urban corridors. Ethnically the region included Kazakhs, Uzbeks, Turkmens, Kyrgyz, Tajiks, Russians, Ukrainians, ethnic Germans, Tatars, Uyghurs, and other minorities

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括国家政治精英、能源和工业管理层、新兴商人阶层、城市知识分子与专业人员、工人、农民、牧民、市场摊贩和失业及半失业人口；苏联解体后社会流动加剧，但贫富差距与地区差异迅速扩大
socialClasses.en: Social classes included state political elites, energy and industrial managers, emerging business strata, urban intellectuals and professionals, workers, peasants, pastoralists, market traders, and unemployed or underemployed populations; social mobility intensified after the Soviet collapse, but inequality and regional disparities widened rapidly

allies.zh: 主要合作对象包括俄罗斯、中国、土耳其、美国、欧盟国家、伊朗以及独联体成员国；在能源、运输和安全问题上采取多向平衡外交
allies.en: Principal partners included Russia, China, Turkey, the United States, EU states, Iran, and fellow CIS members; on energy, transport, and security matters they pursued multi-vector balancing diplomacy

enemies.zh: 不存在统一意义上的国家级公敌，但对跨国伊斯兰武装组织、阿富汗塔利班庇护环境下的渗透网络、毒品集团及部分激进反对派高度警惕
enemies.en: There was no single state-level common enemy, but the republics were highly wary of transnational Islamist armed groups, infiltration networks linked to Taliban-era Afghanistan, narcotics organizations, and certain radical opposition movements

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以主权巩固、政权安全、吸引能源与基础设施投资、维持对俄关系、拓展对华合作并争取西方资本和安全支持为核心；各国在独立自主与大国依赖之间保持谨慎平衡
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on consolidating sovereignty, ensuring regime security, attracting investment in energy and infrastructure, maintaining ties with Russia, expanding cooperation with China, and obtaining Western capital and security support; each state sought a careful balance between independence and dependence on great powers

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术能力集中在苏联遗留的重工业、采矿、油气勘探与管道运输、水电工程、灌溉农业、航天发射基础设施（主要在哈萨克斯坦拜科努尔）、基础通信网络和教育科研体系；但信息技术、制造业升级和民用创新能力总体有限
keyInnovations.en: Key technological capacities were concentrated in inherited Soviet heavy industry, mining, oil and gas exploration and pipelines, hydropower engineering, irrigated agriculture, space-launch infrastructure (especially Baikonur in Kazakhstan), basic telecommunications networks, and educational-scientific institutions; however, information technology, manufacturing upgrading, and civilian innovation remained generally limited

strengths.zh: 优势在于丰富的油气与矿产资源、连接欧亚大陆的战略地理位置、深厚的丝绸之路历史遗产、较高识字率与苏联遗留基础设施，以及在俄罗斯、中国、西方和中东之间进行多边平衡的外交空间
strengths.en: Strengths included abundant oil, gas, and mineral resources, a strategic location linking Eurasia, a deep Silk Road historical legacy, high literacy with inherited Soviet infrastructure, and diplomatic room to balance among Russia, China, the West, and the Middle East

weaknesses.zh: 弱点包括威权政治、制度脆弱、腐败和裙带网络、经济结构单一、内陆封闭地理、跨境水资源争端、民族与地区矛盾、对外部管道和市场依赖，以及吉尔吉斯斯坦和塔吉克斯坦等较贫困成员的财政脆弱性
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included authoritarian politics, fragile institutions, corruption and patronage networks, narrow economic structures, landlocked geography, transboundary water disputes, ethnic and regional tensions, dependence on external pipelines and markets, and the fiscal fragility of poorer members such as Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan

outlook.zh: 至2000年，该地区总体稳定但带有明显脆弱性。若能源价格上升、跨国管道扩张和有限改革持续推进，哈萨克斯坦与土库曼斯坦的增长前景较强，乌兹别克斯坦可凭人口与资源保持区域中心地位；然而若安全局势恶化、威权治理僵化或水资源与边界冲突升级，则整个联合体的长期整合与均衡发展将受到严重限制
outlook.en: By 2000 the region was broadly stable yet clearly fragile. If energy prices rose, transnational pipelines expanded, and limited reforms continued, Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan had strong growth prospects, while Uzbekistan could retain a central regional role through its population and resources; however, worsening security conditions, rigid authoritarian governance, or escalating water and border conflicts would seriously constrain long-term integration and balanced development across the confederation

description.zh: 苏联解体后形成的中亚共和国群，兼具能源、草原与丝路遗产特征。
description.en: A cluster of post-Soviet republics in Central Asia combining energy wealth, steppe geography, and Silk Road legacies.

name.zh: 土耳其与南高加索诸国
土耳其—格鲁吉亚—亚美尼亚—阿塞拜疆区域共同体

ruler.zh: 多位总统：阿赫迈特·内吉代特·塞泽尔、爱德华·谢瓦尔德纳泽、罗伯特·科恰良、盖达尔·阿利耶夫
ruler.en: Various presidents: Ahmet Necdet Sezer, Eduard Shevardnadze, Robert Kocharyan, and Heydar Aliyev

capital.zh: 安卡拉、第比利斯、埃里温、巴库

governmentForm.zh: 由四个主权共和国组成的区域性松散共同体，各国维持独立宪政体制并在安全、贸易、交通与外交议题上有限协调
governmentForm.en: A loose regional commonwealth of four sovereign republics, each retaining its own constitutional system while coordinating in a limited way on security, trade, transport, and diplomacy

socialStructure.zh: 社会由城市中产阶级、国家官僚、军警体系、工商精英、农村居民、工人阶层与流离失所人口共同构成；土耳其社会规模最大且制度较成熟，高加索三国则受转型、战争遗产与民族问题影响更深
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of urban middle classes, state bureaucracies, military and police institutions, business elites, rural populations, workers, and displaced persons; Turkey had the largest and most institutionalized social order, while the three Caucasus republics were more deeply shaped by post-Soviet transition, war legacies, and ethnic issues

rulingClass.zh: 民选总统、议会多数派、总理与内阁、高级司法官员、军方高层、地方权贵及大型商业寡头构成实际统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The effective ruling class was formed by elected presidents, parliamentary majorities, prime ministers and cabinets, senior judicial figures, military high command, regional power brokers, and major business oligarchs

succession.zh: 依各共和国宪法通过总统选举、议会程序及法定任期进行权力更替，但部分国家存在强人政治、舞弊指控或军方间接影响
succession.en: Succession followed constitutional presidential elections, parliamentary procedures, and fixed terms within each republic, though some states experienced strongman politics, allegations of manipulation, or indirect military influence

structure.zh: 整体上无统一中央国家机构，而是四国并行治理；土耳其实行议会共和制并由总统为国家元首、总理主持政府，格鲁吉亚、亚美尼亚和阿塞拜疆为后苏联总统制或半总统制共和国，拥有本国议会、内阁、司法体系与地方行政单位
structure.en: There was no single unified central state, but rather four parallel governments; Turkey operated as a parliamentary republic with a president as head of state and a prime minister heading government, while Georgia, Armenia, and Azerbaijan were post-Soviet presidential or semi-presidential republics with their own parliaments, cabinets, judiciaries, and local administrations

name.en: Foreign Affairs and Regional Cooperation Bodies

function.zh: 负责对外关系、边境谈判、国际组织事务、能源管道合作及与欧洲、俄罗斯、美国和周边中东国家的外交协调
function.en: Managed external relations, border negotiations, international organization affairs, energy pipeline cooperation, and diplomatic coordination with Europe, Russia, the United States, and neighboring Middle Eastern states

function.zh: 负责警察、宪兵、边防、地方行政监督、户籍与公共秩序，特别关注恐怖主义、分离主义与难民安置问题
function.en: Handled police, gendarmerie, border security, supervision of local administration, civil registration, and public order, with special focus on terrorism, separatism, and refugee settlement

function.zh: 负责预算、税收、国有资产、海关、银行监管、私有化与市场改革，土耳其规模最大，高加索三国则高度依赖改革与外援
function.en: Oversaw budgets, taxation, state assets, customs, banking regulation, privatization, and market reforms; Turkey had the largest apparatus, while the Caucasus republics relied heavily on reform programs and external assistance

function.zh: 负责武装力量指挥、军事规划、军需采购、边境防御与同盟合作；其中土耳其军方在政治上具有显著影响力
function.en: Directed armed forces command, military planning, procurement, border defense, and alliance cooperation; the Turkish military in particular retained major political influence

function.zh: 负责法院体系、检察、宪法审查、行政法与选举争议处理，但司法独立程度在四国间差异很大
function.en: Managed courts, prosecution, constitutional review, administrative law, and electoral disputes, though the degree of judicial independence varied widely among the four states

localAdmin.zh: 土耳其以省、区、乡镇为主，由省长和民选地方政府并存；格鲁吉亚、亚美尼亚与阿塞拜疆沿用苏联时期行政区划并进行渐进调整，地方治理能力不均，部分边疆地区受冲突和事实分离政权影响
localAdmin.en: Turkey was organized around provinces, districts, and municipalities, combining appointed governors with elected local governments; Georgia, Armenia, and Azerbaijan largely retained Soviet-era administrative divisions with gradual reforms, and local capacity was uneven, especially in frontier regions affected by conflict and de facto separatist authorities

legalSystem.zh: 土耳其法律体系以大陆法传统与共和宪法为基础；高加索三国则以苏联法制遗产为底并吸收欧洲大陆法模式。整体上强调成文法、宪法法院和国家法典，但执法质量、反腐能力与司法独立水平差异明显
legalSystem.en: Turkey’s legal system was based on continental civil law and republican constitutionalism; the three Caucasus republics rested on Soviet legal legacies modified by European civil-law models. Across the region, codified law, constitutional courts, and state legal codes predominated, but law enforcement quality, anti-corruption capacity, and judicial independence varied sharply

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以增值税、企业税、个人所得税、关税、燃油税与国有资源收入为核心；土耳其税基最广，高加索三国则因经济转型、走私、影子经济与低征管能力而税收效率较低
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on value-added taxes, corporate taxes, personal income taxes, customs duties, fuel taxes, and state resource revenues; Turkey had the broadest tax base, while the Caucasus republics faced weaker collection due to economic transition, smuggling, shadow activity, and limited administrative capacity

religion.zh: 区域宗教格局多元：土耳其与阿塞拜疆以逊尼派和什叶派伊斯兰传统为主但国家体制世俗化；格鲁吉亚与亚美尼亚则以东正教和亚美尼亚使徒教会为民族认同核心。宗教在公共生活和民族政治中具有重要象征作用
religion.en: The region’s religious landscape was diverse: Turkey and Azerbaijan were rooted mainly in Sunni and Shi'a Islamic traditions but maintained secular state structures, while Georgia and Armenia centered national identity on Eastern Orthodoxy and the Armenian Apostolic Church. Religion played an important symbolic role in public life and national politics

philosophy.zh: 主导思想包括凯末尔主义世俗民族国家观、后苏联民族国家建构、市场改革理念、欧洲化取向、国家主权优先以及对历史创伤和民族存续的深刻关切
philosophy.en: Dominant ideas included Kemalist secular nationalism, post-Soviet nation-state building, market reform thinking, Europeanizing aspirations, the primacy of state sovereignty, and deep concern with historical trauma and national survival

writingSystem.zh: 土耳其语使用拉丁字母；阿塞拜疆语在独立后逐步改用拉丁字母；格鲁吉亚语使用本民族格鲁吉亚文字；亚美尼亚语使用亚美尼亚字母。俄语在高加索地区仍广泛作为跨区域交流语言
writingSystem.en: Turkish used the Latin alphabet; Azerbaijani was shifting to a Latin script after independence; Georgian used its native Georgian script; Armenian used the Armenian alphabet. Russian remained widely used in the Caucasus as a regional lingua franca

culturalAchievements.zh: 该区域在2000年前后兼具古老文明遗产与现代国家文化：奥斯曼和安纳托利亚建筑、格鲁吉亚宗教艺术、亚美尼亚修道院传统、阿塞拜疆地毯与音乐、土耳其电影与流行音乐、跨欧亚饮食文化以及不断发展的高等教育和媒体产业均具有代表性
culturalAchievements.en: By 2000 the region combined ancient civilizational heritage with modern national cultures: Ottoman and Anatolian architecture, Georgian religious art, Armenian monastic traditions, Azerbaijani carpets and music, Turkish cinema and popular music, trans-Eurasian culinary culture, and developing higher education and media sectors were all notable achievements

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言分属突厥语系、Kartvelian语系、印欧语系和高加索接触语境；土耳其语和阿塞拜疆语同属突厥语，格鲁吉亚语属南高加索语系，亚美尼亚语属印欧语系
languageFamily.en: The principal languages belonged to Turkic, Kartvelian, and Indo-European families within a dense Caucasian contact zone; Turkish and Azerbaijani were Turkic, Georgian belonged to the South Caucasian/Kartvelian family, and Armenian was Indo-European

gdpDescription.zh: 整体经济以土耳其为绝对主体，高加索三国经济体量较小但在能源、转运与侨汇方面具有战略价值。土耳其已形成较完整的工业、服务业和农业体系，但面临高通胀、财政压力和金融脆弱性；阿塞拜疆受里海油气开发前景推动，格鲁吉亚和亚美尼亚仍处于转型与重建阶段
gdpDescription.en: The aggregate economy was overwhelmingly dominated by Turkey, while the three Caucasus republics were much smaller but strategically significant in energy, transit, and remittances. Turkey had a relatively complete industrial, service, and agricultural structure but faced high inflation, fiscal strain, and financial fragility; Azerbaijan was buoyed by Caspian oil and gas prospects, while Georgia and Armenia remained in transition and reconstruction

mainIndustries.zh: 制造业、纺织服装、食品加工、农业、建筑、旅游、运输、金融服务、石油天然气开发、石化、电力、金属加工、葡萄酒与白兰地、轻工业
mainIndustries.en: Manufacturing, textiles and garments, food processing, agriculture, construction, tourism, transport, financial services, oil and gas development, petrochemicals, electric power, metalworking, wine and brandy, and light industry

tradeGoods.zh: 纺织品、服装、机械设备、食品、烟草、钢铁制品、汽车零部件、地毯、葡萄酒、铜矿产品、原油、成品油、天然气相关产品及农产品
tradeGoods.en: Textiles, garments, machinery, foodstuffs, tobacco, steel goods, automotive components, carpets, wine, copper products, crude oil, refined petroleum, gas-related products, and agricultural produce

name.zh: 土耳其里拉、格鲁吉亚拉里、亚美尼亚德拉姆、阿塞拜疆马纳特
name.en: Turkish lira, Georgian lari, Armenian dram, and Azerbaijani manat

unitName.zh: 里拉、拉里、德拉姆、马纳特

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距很大。土耳其城市家庭通常拥有住房、耐用消费品和小规模储蓄，但受通胀侵蚀严重；高加索家庭更多依赖现金汇款、自给性农业、小商业与非正式经济。战区周边和流离失所者家庭财产水平显著偏低
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Urban Turkish families often owned homes, durable goods, and modest savings, though inflation eroded value; Caucasus households relied more heavily on remittances, subsistence farming, petty commerce, and the informal economy. Families near conflict zones and among the displaced had markedly lower asset levels

economicSystem.zh: 以市场经济为主、国家干预明显的混合经济。土耳其拥有较成熟的私营部门与关税同盟联系；高加索三国在私有化、寡头资本、外援、能源特许经营与国际金融机构条件下推进转型
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on market activity but with substantial state intervention. Turkey possessed a more mature private sector and customs-union links, while the three Caucasus states pursued transition through privatization, oligarchic capital, foreign aid, energy concessions, and programs shaped by international financial institutions

source.zh: 其他费用、罚款与外援性财政收入
source.en: Other fees, fines, and aid-linked fiscal receipts

category.zh: 债务利息与财政融资成本
category.en: Debt interest and fiscal financing costs

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库整体规模中等，但各国状况极不均衡。土耳其具备较强融资能力却深受通胀、债务与短期资本流动影响；阿塞拜疆因能源前景改善财政韧性；格鲁吉亚和亚美尼亚财力有限，更依赖外援、贷款与侨汇支撑
treasuryDescription.en: The combined treasury position was moderate but highly uneven. Turkey had stronger financing capacity yet was burdened by inflation, debt, and short-term capital exposure; Azerbaijan’s fiscal resilience improved with energy prospects; Georgia and Armenia had limited fiscal capacity and depended more on aid, loans, and remittances

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持宏观稳定、偿债、防务开支和基础设施投入为重点。土耳其正努力控制赤字与通胀，高加索三国则在税收现代化、国际援助条件和重建需求之间寻求平衡
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized macroeconomic stability, debt service, defense spending, and infrastructure. Turkey was trying to restrain deficits and inflation, while the Caucasus states balanced tax modernization, conditional international assistance, and reconstruction needs

description.zh: 区域军力核心，以土耳其陆军为绝对主力，拥有机械化步兵、装甲部队、山地部队、宪兵和边防力量；亚美尼亚、阿塞拜疆和格鲁吉亚陆军则重点面向边界防御与地区冲突
description.en: The core of regional military power, dominated overwhelmingly by the Turkish Army with mechanized infantry, armor, mountain forces, gendarmerie, and border units; the Armenian, Azerbaijani, and Georgian armies focused more on border defense and regional conflicts

description.zh: 土耳其海军控制黑海、爱琴海和地中海关键海域并维持两栖和护卫能力；阿塞拜疆在里海拥有有限海军力量，格鲁吉亚海军规模较小，亚美尼亚为内陆国无海军
description.en: The Turkish Navy controlled key waters in the Black Sea, Aegean, and Mediterranean and maintained amphibious and escort capabilities; Azerbaijan possessed a limited Caspian naval force, Georgia maintained a small navy, and landlocked Armenia had no navy

description.zh: 土耳其空军拥有北约标准的战斗机、运输机与防空网络；亚美尼亚和阿塞拜疆维持苏式空防体系与有限航空兵，格鲁吉亚空军较弱
description.en: The Turkish Air Force fielded NATO-standard fighters, transports, and air-defense networks; Armenia and Azerbaijan maintained Soviet-style air defense systems and limited aviation arms, while Georgia’s air arm was weak

description.zh: 包括土耳其宪兵、警备力量、内务部队及高加索各国边防和内卫单位，广泛用于反叛乱、边境管理、反恐和治安控制
description.en: Included the Turkish gendarmerie, security forces, interior troops, and the border and internal security units of the Caucasus states, widely used for counterinsurgency, border management, counterterrorism, and public order

commanderInChief.zh: 各共和国总统为法定武装力量统帅；土耳其实际军事主导权高度集中于总参谋部
commanderInChief.en: The presidents of the respective republics served as legal commanders-in-chief; in Turkey, practical military leadership was highly concentrated in the General Staff

technology.zh: 军事技术水平差异显著。土耳其已深度融入北约体系，装备美制与本土改装平台，具备较强通信、雷达、空军与后勤能力；高加索三国主要依赖苏联遗产装备，维护、训练和现代化水平有限
technology.en: Military technology differed sharply across the region. Turkey was deeply integrated into NATO systems, fielding U.S.-supplied and locally upgraded platforms with strong communications, radar, airpower, and logistics; the three Caucasus republics relied mostly on Soviet legacy equipment with limited maintenance, training, and modernization

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括土耳其东南部库尔德工人党叛乱及跨境渗透、亚美尼亚与阿塞拜疆围绕纳戈尔诺-卡拉巴赫的停火脆弱局势、格鲁吉亚境内阿布哈兹和南奥塞梯分离问题、俄罗斯影响力、美欧地缘竞争以及黑海与高加索能源通道安全风险
threats.en: Main threats included the PKK insurgency and cross-border infiltration in southeastern Turkey, the fragile ceasefire between Armenia and Azerbaijan over Nagorno-Karabakh, separatist issues in Abkhazia and South Ossetia within Georgia, Russian influence, wider Euro-Atlantic versus regional geopolitical competition, and security risks to Black Sea and Caucasus energy corridors

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于土耳其，本区域其余人口分布在格鲁吉亚、亚美尼亚和阿塞拜疆。人口结构年轻，城乡差异明显，民族和语言多样性极高，并包含大量国内流离失所者、战争难民与海外侨民联系网络
populationDescription.en: Most of the population was concentrated in Turkey, with the remainder distributed across Georgia, Armenia, and Azerbaijan. The population was relatively young, marked by strong urban-rural contrasts, very high ethnic and linguistic diversity, and large populations of internally displaced persons, war refugees, and diaspora-linked communities

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括国家官僚与军官集团、城市专业中产、工商业资本家、工人、农民、小商贩、学生群体、宗教和民族社群领袖，以及在高加索冲突背景下形成的退伍军人和流离失所者群体
socialClasses.en: Major social classes included state bureaucratic and military elites, urban professional middle classes, business and commercial capitalists, workers, peasants, petty traders, student populations, religious and ethnic community leaders, and in the Caucasus conflict setting, sizable veteran and displaced populations

allies.zh: 土耳其与北约成员国、美国和欧洲国家关系密切，并与以色列及部分中亚突厥国家保持合作；格鲁吉亚倾向西方并寻求美欧支持；阿塞拜疆与土耳其关系极为紧密并平衡俄罗斯、西方与伊朗；亚美尼亚则与俄罗斯保持战略安全联系，同时维持与伊朗和侨民网络的重要关系
allies.en: Turkey maintained close relations with NATO members, the United States, and European states, and cooperated with Israel and some Turkic Central Asian states; Georgia leaned toward the West and sought U.S. and European support; Azerbaijan was closely aligned with Turkey while balancing Russia, the West, and Iran; Armenia retained a strategic security relationship with Russia alongside important ties to Iran and its global diaspora

enemies.zh: 无统一意义上的共同敌国，但现实对立方包括库尔德工人党等武装组织、亚美尼亚与阿塞拜疆彼此在纳卡问题上的敌对态势、格鲁吉亚与分离地区及其外部支持者之间的持续紧张，以及与希腊、俄罗斯、伊朗和部分邻国在若干议题上的竞争与摩擦
enemies.en: There was no single common enemy, but practical adversaries included armed groups such as the PKK, the hostile Armenia-Azerbaijan relationship over Nagorno-Karabakh, Georgia’s continuing tensions with separatist regions and their external backers, and various strategic frictions with Greece, Russia, Iran, and other neighbors on specific issues

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以地缘平衡为主轴：维护领土完整、争取西方投资与安全支持、控制能源与交通走廊、限制俄罗斯过度影响、处理与伊朗和中东国家的复杂关系，并通过多边组织提升国际地位
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on geopolitical balancing: defending territorial integrity, attracting Western investment and security support, controlling energy and transport corridors, limiting excessive Russian influence, managing complex relations with Iran and Middle Eastern states, and enhancing international standing through multilateral institutions

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术能力包括土耳其较成熟的公路、港口、航空和电信网络，北约标准军事通信系统，里海油气开发技术，巴库—第比利斯—杰伊汉等跨境管道规划，城市大学体系、广播电视媒体扩张以及后苏联国家的银行、海关和行政信息化起步
keyInnovations.en: Key technological capabilities included Turkey’s relatively mature road, port, aviation, and telecommunications networks, NATO-standard military communications, Caspian hydrocarbon development technology, cross-border pipeline planning such as Baku–Tbilisi–Ceyhan, expanding urban university systems and broadcast media, and the early computerization of banking, customs, and administration in the post-Soviet states

strengths.zh: 区位极端重要，控制黑海、安纳托利亚、高加索和里海通道；人口规模大、市场潜力强；土耳其军事和工业基础雄厚；阿塞拜疆能源资源丰富；格鲁吉亚与亚美尼亚在交通、侨民和文化资本方面具有独特优势
strengths.en: The region held an exceptionally strategic position, controlling routes across the Black Sea, Anatolia, the Caucasus, and the Caspian; it had a large population and strong market potential; Turkey possessed substantial military and industrial foundations; Azerbaijan was rich in energy resources; and Georgia and Armenia had distinctive advantages in transit, diaspora links, and cultural capital

weaknesses.zh: 内部缺乏统一政治结构，国家利益相互冲突；高通胀、债务、腐败和制度不均衡明显；纳卡、阿布哈兹、南奥塞梯和库尔德问题持续消耗资源；俄西竞争使安全环境复杂，边境封锁与历史仇恨限制区域合作
weaknesses.en: The region lacked a unified political structure and its state interests often conflicted; inflation, debt, corruption, and uneven institutions were serious; Nagorno-Karabakh, Abkhazia, South Ossetia, and the Kurdish issue consumed resources; and Russian-Western rivalry complicated security while border closures and historical enmities constrained regional cooperation

outlook.zh: 2000年前后前景谨慎乐观但高度不稳定。若能源通道建设、市场改革与对西方整合取得进展，整体实力将明显提升；但若民族冲突复燃、金融危机加剧或大国竞争升级，则区域共同发展将受严重阻碍
outlook.en: Around 2000 the outlook was cautiously hopeful but highly unstable. If energy corridor construction, market reforms, and integration with Western institutions advanced, regional strength would improve significantly; but renewed ethnic conflict, worsening financial crises, or intensified great-power rivalry could seriously impede common development

description.zh: 连接欧洲、中东与里海的战略区域，含土耳其共和国及高加索新独立国家。
description.en: A strategic bridge linking Europe, the Middle East, and the Caspian, including Turkey and the newly independent Caucasus states.

ruler.zh: 阿里·哈梅内伊（最高领袖）／穆罕默德·哈塔米（总统）

governmentForm.zh: 什叶派教法监护下的神权共和制，结合民选机构与宗教监督机构
governmentForm.en: A Shi'a theocratic republic under Guardianship of the Islamic Jurist, combining elected institutions with clerical supervisory bodies

socialStructure.zh: 社会由什叶派教士阶层、革命机构精英、国家官僚、中产城市阶层、巴扎商人、工人、农民及少数民族和宗教群体构成；城市化与教育扩张推动社会流动，但政治权力高度集中于宗教—革命体制核心
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the Shi'a clerical establishment, revolutionary institutional elites, state bureaucrats, an expanding urban middle class, bazaar merchants, workers, peasants, and ethnic and religious minorities; urbanization and education widened social mobility, but political power remained concentrated in the clerical-revolutionary core

rulingClass.zh: 最高领袖周围的高级教士、宪法监护机构、革命卫队高层、司法与安全机关领导层，以及与巴扎和宗教基金会相连的保守派政治精英
rulingClass.en: Senior clerics around the Supreme Leader, constitutional oversight bodies, the upper command of the Revolutionary Guards, leaders of the judiciary and security organs, and conservative political elites linked to the bazaar and religious foundations

succession.zh: 最高领袖由专家会议选举并理论上可罢免；总统由全民选举产生但须经监护委员会审查候选资格；议会亦由选举产生并受神权机构监督
succession.en: The Supreme Leader was selected by the Assembly of Experts and in theory could be dismissed by it; the President was elected by popular vote but candidates required vetting by the Guardian Council; parliament was also elected but remained subject to clerical oversight

structure.zh: 国家权力由最高领袖居于核心，上有专家会议与监护委员会等宗教宪制机关，下有总统、内阁、伊斯兰议会、司法系统、地方行政体系，以及革命卫队与正规军并行的安全架构
structure.en: State power centered on the Supreme Leader, with religious-constitutional bodies such as the Assembly of Experts and Guardian Council above or alongside the elected presidency, cabinet, Islamic Consultative Assembly, judiciary, local administration, and a dual security structure of Revolutionary Guards and regular armed forces

function.zh: 负责行政管理、经济政策执行、国家预算编制、发展规划与国内公共事务协调
function.en: Responsible for executive administration, implementation of economic policy, budget preparation, development planning, and coordination of domestic public affairs

function.zh: 负责立法、预算审议、质询内阁，并在监护委员会审查下处理公共政策
function.en: Responsible for legislation, budget review, questioning the cabinet, and handling public policy subject to Guardian Council review

function.zh: 执行伊斯兰共和国法律体系，管理法院、检察、监狱和法律监督事务
function.en: Administered the legal system of the Islamic Republic, including courts, prosecution, prisons, and legal supervision

name.zh: 内政部与地方行政系统
name.en: Ministry of Interior and Local Administration

function.zh: 管理省、县、区行政，组织选举，维护内部秩序并监督地方治理
function.en: Managed provincial, county, and district administration, organized elections, maintained internal order, and supervised local governance

function.zh: 负责外交关系、国际谈判、地区政策和驻外使团管理
function.en: Handled diplomatic relations, international negotiations, regional policy, and management of overseas missions

name.zh: 石油部与经济管理机构
name.en: Ministry of Petroleum and Economic Management Bodies

function.zh: 管理油气资源开发、出口、能源收入及与国家发展相关的宏观经济协调
function.en: Managed hydrocarbon development, exports, energy revenues, and macroeconomic coordination tied to national development

function.zh: 包括监护委员会、国家利益委员会、专家会议等，负责教法与宪法审查、体制仲裁及领导监督
function.en: Included the Guardian Council, Expediency Council, Assembly of Experts and related bodies, responsible for religious-constitutional review, regime arbitration, and leadership oversight

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为多个省，由中央任命或批准的省长与地方官员治理；县、区、乡镇构成层级行政网络，地方议会在部分市政事务上发挥作用，但安全与关键权力仍掌握于中央及内政体系
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into provinces governed by centrally appointed or approved governors and local officials; counties, districts, and townships formed a hierarchical administrative network, while local councils played roles in some municipal affairs, though security and key authority remained in central hands

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合大陆法传统、伊斯兰法特别是十二伊玛目派什叶法学原则，以及革命后制定的宪法与成文法；家庭法、刑法和公共道德受宗教法显著影响
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined civil-law traditions, Islamic law—especially Twelver Shi'a jurisprudence—and the post-revolutionary constitution and statutory law; family law, criminal law, and public morality were strongly shaped by religious jurisprudence

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以石油收入、关税、企业税、所得税、消费税和各类行政收费为主；非税收入和国有部门收益占重要地位，宗教基金会与准国有机构享有特殊影响力
taxationSystem.en: Public finance relied primarily on oil revenues, customs duties, corporate taxes, income taxes, consumption taxes, and administrative fees; non-tax revenues and earnings from the state sector were significant, while religious foundations and quasi-state bodies held special influence

religion.zh: 国教与主导宗教为十二伊玛目派什叶伊斯兰教，同时境内存在逊尼派穆斯林、基督徒、犹太教徒、琐罗亚斯德教徒及其他少数群体
religion.en: The state religion and dominant faith was Twelver Shi'a Islam, while the country also contained Sunni Muslims, Christians, Jews, Zoroastrians, and other minority communities

philosophy.zh: 思想生活结合伊斯兰法学、什叶派政治神学、伊朗民族文化传统、革命意识形态以及围绕改革、法治、公民社会与宗教权威关系的激烈公共讨论
philosophy.en: Intellectual life combined Islamic jurisprudence, Shi'a political theology, Iranian national cultural traditions, revolutionary ideology, and intense public debate over reform, rule of law, civil society, and the relationship between religion and authority

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用波斯语文字，采用改造后的阿拉伯字母；国家行政、教育、文学与媒体均以波斯文为主，少数民族语言在地方与文化领域有限使用
writingSystem.en: The primary script was Persian written in a modified Arabic alphabet; state administration, education, literature, and media used Persian predominantly, while minority languages had limited use in local and cultural settings

culturalAchievements.zh: 伊朗在诗歌、电影、宗教建筑、地毯编织、传统音乐、哲学与历史学方面具有深厚传统；至2000年，伊朗电影已在国际影坛获得显著声誉，现代文学、出版与大学教育亦十分活跃
culturalAchievements.en: Iran possessed deep traditions in poetry, cinema, religious architecture, carpet weaving, traditional music, philosophy, and historiography; by 2000 Iranian cinema had gained major international recognition, while modern literature, publishing, and university education were also vibrant

languageFamily.zh: 官方语言波斯语属于印欧语系伊朗语支；境内另有阿塞拜疆语、库尔德语、阿拉伯语、俾路支语、土库曼语等多种语言
languageFamily.en: The official language, Persian, belonged to the Iranian branch of the Indo-European family; the country also contained Azerbaijani, Kurdish, Arabic, Balochi, Turkmen, and other languages

gdpDescription.zh: 2000年前后的伊朗经济属中等收入发展中经济体，规模在中东地区居前列。经济高度依赖石油和天然气出口，国家干预较强，存在补贴体系、价格管制、失业压力与金融效率不足等问题，但工业基础、教育水平和国内市场规模赋予其显著韧性
gdpDescription.en: Around 2000, Iran was a middle-income developing economy and one of the larger economies in the Middle East. It depended heavily on oil and gas exports, featured strong state intervention, and faced subsidies, price controls, unemployment pressure, and weak financial efficiency, but its industrial base, educational level, and large domestic market gave it significant resilience

mainIndustries.zh: 石油与天然气开采、炼油与石化、汽车装配、钢铁、水泥、纺织、食品加工、建筑业、电力、传统手工业与地毯业
mainIndustries.en: Oil and gas extraction, refining and petrochemicals, automobile assembly, steel, cement, textiles, food processing, construction, electricity, and traditional handicrafts including carpets

tradeGoods.zh: 主要出口原油、石油产品、天然气相关产品、石化产品、地毯、干果、矿产品和部分工业品；主要进口机械设备、交通工具、工业零部件、药品、化工品和消费品
tradeGoods.en: Major exports included crude oil, petroleum products, gas-related products, petrochemicals, carpets, dried fruits, minerals, and some industrial goods; major imports included machinery, transport equipment, industrial parts, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and consumer goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异显著。德黑兰及主要城市的中产家庭拥有住房、教育资本和耐用品，但通货膨胀、青年失业和收入分配不均削弱购买力；农村及边缘省份家庭财富较低，部分依赖国家补贴、农业和汇款
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply. Middle-class families in Tehran and major cities often held housing, educational capital, and durable goods, but inflation, youth unemployment, and unequal income distribution weakened purchasing power; rural and peripheral provinces had lower household wealth and relied more on subsidies, agriculture, and remittances

economicSystem.zh: 混合经济，以国家控制能源部门和关键工业为核心，同时存在私营巴扎经济、合作部门、准国有宗教基金会以及受监管的市场活动
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on state control of the energy sector and key industries, alongside a private bazaar economy, cooperative sectors, quasi-state religious foundations, and regulated market activity

source.zh: 企业税与国有部门收益

treasuryDescription.zh: 国家财政储备有限但并不空虚，依赖油价波动与外汇收入状况。政府维持一定外汇与主权资金缓冲，但受制裁压力、预算刚性和补贴负担影响，财政弹性受限
treasuryDescription.en: State reserves were limited but not empty, and depended heavily on oil prices and foreign-exchange inflows. The government maintained some foreign-exchange and sovereign buffers, but sanctions pressure, budget rigidities, and subsidy burdens constrained fiscal flexibility

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策在维持社会稳定、补贴基本消费、资助革命机构与保障国防之间寻求平衡；其核心弱点是对石油收入依赖过深，导致预算随国际能源市场大幅波动
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy sought to balance social stability, subsidies for basic consumption, support for revolutionary institutions, and defense needs; its central weakness was deep dependence on oil revenue, causing major exposure to fluctuations in global energy markets

name.zh: 伊朗伊斯兰共和国军队地面部队
name.en: Islamic Republic of Iran Army Ground Forces

description.zh: 正规陆军主力，负责边境防御、装甲和机械化作战、山地与内陆防务，装备多为美制旧装与后续国产或改装平台
description.en: The main regular land force, responsible for border defense, armored and mechanized warfare, and mountain and inland defense, using a mix of older U.S.-supplied equipment and later domestic or modified platforms

name.zh: 伊朗伊斯兰共和国空军
name.en: Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force

description.zh: 承担空防、拦截、有限对地打击与战略威慑支持任务，机队老化但维护能力较强
description.en: Handled air defense, interception, limited ground attack, and support for strategic deterrence, with an aging but sustained fleet

name.zh: 伊朗伊斯兰共和国海军

description.zh: 负责阿曼湾与部分远海存在，拥有小型水面舰艇、潜艇和海上巡逻力量
description.en: Responsible for the Gulf of Oman and limited blue-water presence, with small surface combatants, submarines, and maritime patrol assets

description.zh: 革命政权核心武装，兼具军事、政治与经济影响力，负责体制保卫、导弹力量、精锐地面部队和部分对外战略行动
description.en: The core armed institution of the revolutionary regime, wielding military, political, and economic influence, responsible for regime protection, missile forces, elite ground units, and parts of external strategic activity

name.zh: 巴斯基民兵与动员力量

description.zh: 作为准军事动员网络服务于内部安全、社会控制、后备补充和意识形态动员
description.en: Served as a paramilitary mobilization network for internal security, social control, reserve support, and ideological mobilization

commanderInChief.zh: 最高领袖阿里·哈梅内伊

technology.zh: 军事技术总体处于中等水平，特点是装备来源复杂、维护与仿制能力较强、导弹与不对称作战能力突出，但空军与海军重装备现代化受制裁限制明显
technology.en: Military technology was broadly moderate in level, marked by mixed equipment origins, strong maintenance and reverse-engineering capacity, and notable missile and asymmetric warfare capabilities, while modernization of heavy air and naval assets was clearly constrained by sanctions

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括与伊拉克长期敌对的余波、美国在海湾地区的军事存在、以色列的战略对抗、塔利班控制下阿富汗东部边境不稳，以及波斯湾海上通道安全与国内分离主义和反政府武装风险
threats.en: Main threats included the legacy of long hostility with Iraq, the U.S. military presence in the Gulf, strategic confrontation with Israel, instability on the eastern border with Taliban-ruled Afghanistan, and risks to Persian Gulf sea lanes as well as domestic separatist and anti-state militancy

populationDescription.zh: 2000年的伊朗人口年轻且增长较快，教育普及率上升，城市化持续推进。波斯人为主体，另有阿塞拜疆人、库尔德人、卢尔人、阿拉伯人、俾路支人、土库曼人及其他群体，整体人口结构多元而以国家整合为主
populationDescription.en: In 2000 Iran had a youthful and still relatively fast-growing population, rising educational attainment, and continuing urbanization. Persians formed the majority, with Azerbaijanis, Kurds, Lurs, Arabs, Baloch, Turkmen, and others making up a diverse population integrated primarily through the state

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括高级教士与革命精英、国家官员与专业中产、巴扎商人、工人阶层、农民与边缘地区居民。教育扩张催生庞大的学生和青年群体，他们在文化与政治上更具活力并成为改革与保守力量竞争的焦点
socialClasses.en: Social strata included senior clerics and revolutionary elites, state officials and the professional middle class, bazaar merchants, the working class, peasants, and residents of peripheral regions. Expanded education created a large student and youth cohort that was more culturally and politically dynamic and became a key arena of contest between reformist and conservative forces

allies.zh: 伊朗在2000年并无正式意义上的广泛军事同盟，但与叙利亚保持紧密战略关系，并与黎巴嫩真主党、部分巴勒斯坦组织以及俄罗斯、中国和若干中亚、海湾及欧亚国家维持不同层次的合作
allies.en: In 2000 Iran did not possess a broad network of formal military allies, but maintained a close strategic relationship with Syria and varying degrees of cooperation with Hezbollah in Lebanon, some Palestinian groups, as well as Russia, China, and several Central Asian, Gulf, and Eurasian states

enemies.zh: 主要敌对或高度紧张对象包括美国、以色列以及与其存在地缘和意识形态竞争的部分海湾阿拉伯国家；与塔利班政权关系亦极为紧张
enemies.en: Its principal adversarial or highly tense relationships included the United States, Israel, and some Gulf Arab states with which it had geopolitical and ideological rivalry; relations with the Taliban regime were also extremely tense

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策兼具革命意识形态、国家安全考量与现实主义平衡。伊朗寻求维护海湾影响力、抵制美国遏制、支持地区代理力量、发展与欧亚大国及欧洲的经贸联系，并在哈塔米时期尝试以“文明对话”改善国际形象
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined revolutionary ideology, national security concerns, and pragmatic balancing. Iran sought to maintain influence in the Gulf, resist U.S. containment, support regional proxy partners, deepen trade and strategic ties with Eurasian powers and Europe, and under Khatami attempted to improve its international image through a 'Dialogue Among Civilizations'

keyInnovations.zh: 伊朗在导弹研发、军工仿制、石化工程、水坝与基础设施建设、医学教育、核技术基础研究和电影制作方面具有较突出能力；互联网与移动通信开始扩展，但高端技术获取受国际制裁限制
keyInnovations.en: Iran showed notable capability in missile development, defense-industrial reverse engineering, petrochemical engineering, dam and infrastructure construction, medical education, foundational nuclear research, and filmmaking; internet and mobile communications were beginning to spread, though access to high-end technology was constrained by international sanctions

strengths.zh: 伊朗的优势在于庞大人口、强烈国家认同、深厚文明传统、丰富油气资源、较强教育体系、区域战略纵深以及兼具正规与非对称手段的安全能力
strengths.en: Iran's strengths lay in its large population, strong state identity, deep civilizational tradition, abundant oil and gas resources, relatively strong educational system, regional strategic depth, and security capacity combining regular and asymmetric means

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括经济对油气出口依赖过深、青年失业和通胀问题、国际制裁压力、政治派系内耗、制度双重结构导致决策低效，以及民族与地区发展不平衡
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included excessive dependence on hydrocarbon exports, youth unemployment and inflation, pressure from international sanctions, factional political infighting, inefficiency from dual institutional structures, and uneven ethnic and regional development

outlook.zh: 2000年的伊朗总体稳定并具上升潜力。若改革派能够在不触动体制核心的情况下推进法治、开放和经济调整，国家有望提升国际地位；但若保守派与外部对抗加剧，伊朗将继续在内外压力中维持一种受限而坚韧的区域强权地位
outlook.en: In 2000 Iran was broadly stable and possessed upward potential. If reformists could advance rule of law, openness, and economic adjustment without overturning the regime core, the country could improve its international standing; but if conservative resistance and external confrontation intensified, Iran would continue to hold a constrained yet resilient position as a regional power under mounting internal and external pressure

description.zh: 以什叶派神权共和体制治理的地区强国，在改革与保守之间博弈。
description.en: A regional power governed by a Shi'a theocratic-republican system, balancing reformist and conservative currents.

governmentForm.zh: 高度集权的总统制共和国，由阿拉伯复兴社会党主导的一党威权体制
governmentForm.en: A highly centralized presidential republic under a one-party authoritarian system dominated by the Arab Socialist Ba'ath Party

socialStructure.zh: 国家以城市官僚、军队、安全机构和党组织为核心，社会由执政党精英、军政干部、部族网络、城市中产阶层、工人和广大农村人口组成；在制裁压力下，国家配给体系对社会维持具有关键作用。
socialStructure.en: The state was centered on urban bureaucracy, the military, security institutions, and party structures; society consisted of ruling party elites, military-administrative cadres, tribal networks, urban middle strata, workers, and a large rural population. Under sanctions, the state rationing system was crucial to social maintenance.

rulingClass.zh: 复兴党高层、总统家族与提克里特系亲信、安全机构领导层、共和国卫队指挥层和国家官僚精英
rulingClass.en: Senior Ba'ath Party officials, the presidential family and Tikriti loyalists, leaders of the security apparatus, the Republican Guard command, and the state bureaucratic elite

succession.zh: 法律上由总统体制和革命指挥委员会安排，实际上依赖总统个人指定与家族—党内权力平衡，带有强烈个人统治色彩
succession.en: Formally structured through the presidential system and Revolutionary Command Council framework, but in practice dependent on personal designation by the president and family-party power balancing, with a strongly personalist character

structure.zh: 中央政权由总统、部长会议、名义上的国民议会、复兴党地区领导机构及安全机关构成；总统通过党、军队和情报体系实施最终控制，地方行政由各省总督和中央任命官员执行。
structure.en: The central government consisted of the presidency, Council of Ministers, a nominal National Assembly, regional Ba'ath Party leadership organs, and security agencies; the president exercised ultimate control through the party, military, and intelligence apparatus, while provincial administration was carried out by governors and centrally appointed officials.

name.zh: 总统府与革命指挥核心
name.en: Presidential Diwan and Revolutionary Command Core

function.zh: 统筹国家最高决策、干部任命、战略指令与政权协调
function.en: Coordinated top state decision-making, appointments, strategic directives, and regime management

function.zh: 负责警察、国内治安、户籍管理、边境与内部控制
function.en: Handled police, internal security, civil registration, borders, and internal control

function.zh: 管理正规军、军事规划、后勤与国防行政
function.en: Managed the regular armed forces, military planning, logistics, and defense administration

function.zh: 负责预算、税收、国库、对外付款和财政调度
function.en: Responsible for budgeting, taxation, the treasury, foreign payments, and fiscal management

function.zh: 管理石油开采、炼制、出口安排及石油换食品计划下的产销协调
function.en: Oversaw oil extraction, refining, export arrangements, and production-marketing coordination under the Oil-for-Food Programme

function.zh: 负责国家配给体系、进口审批和民生物资分配
function.en: Managed the state rationing system, import approvals, and distribution of essential goods

function.zh: 负责医院系统、公共卫生和药品配给管理
function.en: Responsible for hospitals, public health, and medicine distribution management

function.zh: 管理基础教育、课程、学校行政和扫盲项目
function.en: Managed basic education, curricula, school administration, and literacy programs

name.en: Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research

function.zh: 负责大学、专业学院和科研机构的行政管理
function.en: Oversaw universities, specialized institutes, and research institutions

function.zh: 监控政治忠诚、打击反对派、开展国内外情报活动
function.en: Monitored political loyalty, suppressed opposition, and conducted domestic and foreign intelligence activities

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为多个省份，由中央任命或严格控制的省长和地方党组织管理；地方行政在形式上承担公共服务职能，实际上受安全机关和党系统强力监督。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into governorates administered by centrally appointed or tightly controlled governors and local party organizations; local administration formally handled public services but in practice operated under strong supervision by security agencies and the party system.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以大陆法传统、阿拉伯法典化立法和部分伊斯兰法原则为基础，但政治案件和国家安全事务常被特别法、革命法庭和行政命令主导，司法独立性有限。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on civil-law traditions, codified Arab legislation, and some Islamic legal principles, but political cases and state-security matters were often dominated by special laws, revolutionary courts, and executive orders, with limited judicial independence.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要依赖石油，税制在制裁时期相对次要，包括关税、国有企业收入、工资税费和各类行政收费；配给与价格管制在实际经济管理中比常规税制更重要。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal revenue depended primarily on oil, while the tax system was secondary during the sanctions era, including customs duties, state-enterprise receipts, payroll taxes, and various administrative fees; rationing and price controls were more important than ordinary taxation in actual economic management.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教占主导地位，人口中什叶派与逊尼派并存，另有基督徒、雅兹迪人和其他少数宗教群体；国家在1990年代后期强化伊斯兰象征，但政权本质上仍以世俗阿拉伯民族主义为基础。
religion.en: Islam was the dominant religion, with both Shi'a and Sunni populations, alongside Christians, Yazidis, and other minority communities; the state intensified Islamic symbolism in the late 1990s, but the regime remained fundamentally grounded in secular Arab nationalism.

philosophy.zh: 官方意识形态为阿拉伯民族主义、国家主义和复兴党式社会主义，强调统一、反帝、秩序和领袖崇拜，同时在制裁时期吸收更多保守宗教修辞以强化合法性。
philosophy.en: The official ideology was Arab nationalism, statism, and Ba'athist socialism, emphasizing unity, anti-imperialism, order, and leader veneration, while during the sanctions period it incorporated more conservative religious rhetoric to reinforce legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语，库尔德地区亦使用阿拉伯字母改写的库尔德文；国家文书、教育和媒体以标准阿拉伯语为主。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script was primarily used to write Arabic, while Kurdish in some regions was also written in adapted Arabic script; state documents, education, and media relied mainly on Standard Arabic.

culturalAchievements.zh: 伊拉克继承美索不达米亚古文明与阿拉伯—伊斯兰学术传统，在文学、诗歌、历史研究、建筑遗产和考古领域具有深厚积累；尽管制裁严重削弱文化机构，巴格达仍保持阿拉伯世界重要文化中心之一的地位。
culturalAchievements.en: Iraq inherited the ancient Mesopotamian and Arab-Islamic scholarly traditions and possessed deep achievements in literature, poetry, historical studies, architectural heritage, and archaeology; despite sanctions severely weakening cultural institutions, Baghdad remained one of the major cultural centers of the Arab world.

languageFamily.zh: 官方主导语言为闪米特语系的阿拉伯语；北部广泛使用印欧语系伊朗语族的库尔德语，另有土库曼语、叙利亚语新阿拉姆语等少数语言。
languageFamily.en: The dominant official language was Arabic of the Semitic family; Kurdish, from the Iranian branch of the Indo-European family, was widely spoken in the north, alongside minority languages such as Turkmen and Neo-Aramaic varieties.

gdpDescription.zh: 2000年伊拉克经济受联合国制裁、战争破坏和基础设施老化严重制约，但因油价回升与石油换食品计划扩大，产出较1990年代中期有所改善。经济结构极度依赖石油出口和国家分配体系，私人部门规模有限。
gdpDescription.en: In 2000, Iraq's economy was heavily constrained by UN sanctions, war damage, and aging infrastructure, but output improved relative to the mid-1990s due to higher oil prices and the expansion of the Oil-for-Food Programme. The economic structure was extremely dependent on oil exports and state distribution, with only a limited private sector.

mainIndustries.zh: 石油开采与炼制、国有制造业、食品加工、建筑材料、化工、农业、交通运输和国家配给相关商业
mainIndustries.en: Oil extraction and refining, state manufacturing, food processing, construction materials, chemicals, agriculture, transport, and commerce tied to the state rationing system

tradeGoods.zh: 主要出口原油及少量石油产品；主要进口粮食、药品、医疗设备、机械零件、工业投入品和消费必需品
tradeGoods.en: Main exports were crude oil and limited petroleum products; major imports included grain, medicines, medical equipment, machine parts, industrial inputs, and essential consumer goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富在制裁下大幅缩水，城市中产阶层资产被通胀和收入崩溃侵蚀；多数家庭依赖政府口粮配给、非正式市场和亲属网络维持生活。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was sharply reduced under sanctions, and the assets of the urban middle class were eroded by inflation and collapsing incomes; most households relied on government food rations, informal markets, and kinship networks to sustain themselves.

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导、石油依赖、受制裁约束的配给型混合经济；价格、进口和大宗分配由国家高度控制，非正式市场在民生层面占有重要地位。
economicSystem.en: A state-dominated, oil-dependent, sanctions-constrained rationing-based mixed economy; prices, imports, and major distribution channels were tightly controlled by the state, while the informal market played a major role in everyday survival.

source.en: Domestic taxes, fees, and other revenue

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况脆弱，现金流高度依赖受国际监督的石油收入。虽然政权保有一定外汇和贵重储备，但可自由调度空间有限，财政运转常受制裁机制和紧急安全支出挤压。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was fragile, with cash flow highly dependent on internationally supervised oil revenues. Although the regime retained some foreign exchange and valuable reserves, its freely deployable resources were limited, and fiscal operations were often squeezed by sanctions mechanisms and urgent security spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持政权稳定、保障军警体系和最低限度民生配给为核心，投资偏重关键基础设施修补与忠诚网络维系，而非全面市场改革。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on regime stability, sustaining the military-police apparatus, and preserving minimum subsistence rationing; investment was directed toward repairing critical infrastructure and maintaining loyalty networks rather than comprehensive market reform.

description.zh: 正规地面部队，为全国防御与内部镇压的主体力量，装备大量老旧苏式与部分西方来源武器，战备和后勤受制裁严重影响。
description.en: The regular ground force and main instrument for national defense and internal repression, equipped with many aging Soviet-style and some Western-origin weapons; readiness and logistics were heavily affected by sanctions.

name.zh: 共和国卫队与特别共和国卫队
name.en: Republican Guard and Special Republican Guard

description.zh: 政权最可靠的精锐部队，负责首都防卫、战略预备和政权安全，待遇与装备优于一般部队。
description.en: The regime's most reliable elite forces, responsible for defending the capital, serving as a strategic reserve, and protecting the regime; they received better pay and equipment than ordinary units.

name.en: Air Defense Forces and Air Force

description.zh: 由地空导弹、雷达和有限的作战飞机组成，在禁飞区和长期打击下能力受限，但仍维持象征性防空网络。
description.en: Composed of surface-to-air missiles, radar, and a limited combat aircraft inventory; its capabilities were constrained by no-fly zones and repeated strikes, though it still maintained a symbolic air defense network.

description.zh: 规模很小，主要负责近岸巡逻、河口与港口保卫，海上能力有限。
description.en: Very small in scale, mainly tasked with coastal patrols and protection of estuaries and ports, with limited maritime capability.

description.zh: 包括边防、民兵和辅助安全力量，用于边境监控、设施警戒和内部维稳。
description.en: Included border guards, militias, and auxiliary security forces used for border monitoring, facility protection, and internal stability operations.

commanderInChief.zh: 总统兼武装部队总司令萨达姆·侯赛因

technology.zh: 军备以20世纪70至80年代获取和国产化维护的苏制、法制及混合装备为主，包括T-55、T-62、T-72坦克、老式米格与幻影战机、防空导弹、火炮和弹道导弹残余能力；制裁导致零件短缺、训练不足和系统老化。
technology.en: The armed forces relied mainly on Soviet, French, and mixed equipment acquired in the 1970s-1980s and sustained through local maintenance, including T-55, T-62, and T-72 tanks, older MiG and Mirage aircraft, air-defense missiles, artillery, and residual ballistic missile capability; sanctions caused spare-parts shortages, inadequate training, and systemic aging.

threats.zh: 主要外部威胁来自美国及其盟友在海湾地区的军事压力、英美空袭与禁飞区执法，以及与伊朗、科威特相关的长期战略对立；内部则面临库尔德地区离心、什叶派不满和政变恐惧。
threats.en: Major external threats came from US and allied military pressure in the Gulf, Anglo-American air strikes and no-fly-zone enforcement, and the long-term strategic antagonism tied to Iran and Kuwait; internally, the regime faced Kurdish autonomy pressures, Shi'a discontent, and fear of coups.

populationDescription.zh: 2000年伊拉克人口约2400万，主体为阿拉伯人，北部有大规模库尔德人口，另有土库曼人、亚述人、亚美尼亚人等少数族群。人口年龄结构年轻，但长期制裁、战争创伤和公共卫生恶化带来明显社会压力。
populationDescription.en: Iraq's population in 2000 was about 24 million, predominantly Arab with a large Kurdish population in the north, alongside Turkmen, Assyrians, Armenians, and other minorities. The age structure was young, but prolonged sanctions, wartime trauma, and deteriorating public health created severe social strain.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括执政党—安全精英、军官与官僚、依赖国家工资的城市中产与专业人士、工人和小商贩、部族和农村农民、以及在制裁下扩大的贫困群体。地区、宗派和族群差异对资源分配和政治地位具有重要影响。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included the ruling party-security elite, officers and bureaucrats, urban middle and professional groups dependent on state salaries, workers and petty traders, tribal and rural peasant populations, and a growing impoverished population under sanctions. Regional, sectarian, and ethnic differences strongly shaped resource access and political status.

allies.zh: 伊拉克在2000年缺乏正式稳固盟友，但与约旦保持重要经济联系，并与部分阿拉伯国家、俄罗斯、法国和中国维持务实外交往来，争取缓解制裁与恢复地区地位。
allies.en: Iraq lacked firm formal allies in 2000, but maintained important economic ties with Jordan and pragmatic diplomatic relations with some Arab states, Russia, France, and China in an effort to ease sanctions and restore regional standing.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象为美国、英国和执行对伊遏制政策的西方联盟；与科威特和伊朗关系仍受战争与边界遗留问题影响，并与以色列保持强烈敌对立场。
enemies.en: Its main adversaries were the United States, the United Kingdom, and the Western coalition enforcing containment against Iraq; relations with Kuwait and Iran remained shaped by war and border legacies, and Iraq maintained a strongly hostile posture toward Israel.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以突破制裁、维持政权生存、反对美国主导的地区秩序、强化阿拉伯民族主义姿态并利用国际分歧争取战略空间为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on breaking sanctions, ensuring regime survival, opposing the US-led regional order, projecting Arab nationalist leadership, and exploiting international divisions to gain strategic room.

keyInnovations.zh: 伊拉克拥有较完整但受损严重的石油工业体系、电力与灌溉工程、导弹与军工维护能力、大学教育网络和国家级医疗体系基础。到2000年，这些技术与制度能力多因战争、制裁和人才流失而衰退，但在区域内仍保留一定工业和工程基础。
keyInnovations.en: Iraq possessed a relatively comprehensive but badly damaged oil industry, power and irrigation infrastructure, missile and military-industrial maintenance capabilities, a university network, and the foundations of a national medical system. By 2000, many of these technical and institutional capacities had declined due to war, sanctions, and brain drain, but the country still retained a meaningful industrial and engineering base by regional standards.

strengths.zh: 伊拉克的优势在于丰富的石油资源、美索不达米亚核心地带的农业与交通地理、长期形成的国家官僚体系、较大的城市人口基础，以及在高压环境下仍能运作的配给和治安控制网络。
strengths.en: Iraq's strengths lay in its abundant oil resources, the agricultural and transport geography of the Mesopotamian core, a long-developed state bureaucracy, a large urban population base, and rationing and security-control networks that continued functioning under severe pressure.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是国际制裁、基础设施破败、经济单一依赖石油、政权合法性脆弱、族群与宗派裂痕、军备老化以及长期对外对抗造成的外交孤立。
weaknesses.en: Its principal weaknesses were international sanctions, decayed infrastructure, overdependence on oil, fragile regime legitimacy, ethnic and sectarian fractures, aging military equipment, and diplomatic isolation caused by prolonged confrontation with external powers.

outlook.zh: 到2000年，伊拉克政权短期内仍具维持统治的强制能力，但中长期前景危险而不稳定；若制裁持续、与美国对抗升级且内部矛盾累积，国家将面临更深层的经济衰退、政治危机甚至外部军事打击风险。
outlook.en: By 2000, the Iraqi regime still possessed enough coercive capacity to maintain rule in the short term, but its medium- and long-term outlook was dangerous and unstable; if sanctions persisted, confrontation with the United States escalated, and internal tensions accumulated, the country faced risks of deeper economic decline, political crisis, and even external military attack.

description.zh: 在制裁与地区对抗下维持的威权国家，控制两河流域核心。
description.en: An authoritarian state under sanctions and regional confrontation, controlling the Mesopotamian core.

ideology.zh: 库尔德民族主义、联邦自治

controlledArea.zh: 伊拉克北部库尔德自治区，包括埃尔比勒、苏莱曼尼亚、杜胡克三省
controlledArea.en: Kurdistan Autonomous Region in northern Iraq, including Erbil, Sulaymaniyah, and Duhok provinces

description.zh: 库尔德自卫武装（佩什梅格），装备轻重武器，在反ISIS战争中获得国际援助
description.en: Kurdish Peshmerga self-defense forces, equipped with light and heavy weapons, received international support during anti-ISIS campaign

relationship.zh: 联邦框架下的高度自治。拥有独立的议会、政府和武装力量，与巴格达中央政府在石油收入分配和领土争议上存在持续紧张关系。
relationship.en: Highly autonomous within the federal framework. Has independent parliament, government, and armed forces, with persistent tensions with Baghdad over oil revenue sharing and territorial disputes.

description.zh: 伊拉克联邦内的库尔德自治地区，2003年萨达姆倒台后获得实质独立地位。拥有发达的石油经济和相对稳定的安全环境。
description.en: The Kurdish autonomous region within federal Iraq, achieving de facto independence after Saddam's fall in 2003. Has a developed oil economy and relatively stable security environment.

name.zh: 什叶派民兵联盟（人民动员力量）

ideology.zh: 什叶派伊斯兰主义、亲伊朗

description.zh: 受伊朗革命卫队支持的什叶派武装团体联盟，名义上编入伊拉克安全部队
description.en: Coalition of Shia armed groups supported by Iran's IRGC, nominally integrated into Iraqi security forces

relationship.zh: 名义上隶属伊拉克政府，实际上受伊朗深度影响。在反ISIS战争中发挥关键作用后，政治影响力大增。
relationship.en: Nominally under the Iraqi government but deeply influenced by Iran. Political influence greatly increased after playing a key role in the anti-ISIS war.

description.zh: 2014年为对抗ISIS而组建的什叶派准军事力量联盟，由数十个武装团体组成。已成为伊拉克政治和军事格局中的重要力量。
description.en: A coalition of Shia paramilitary forces formed in 2014 to combat ISIS, comprising dozens of armed groups. Has become a major force in Iraq's political and military landscape.

name.en: Western Europe and the Core EU States
Western Europe and the Core European Union Confederation

ruler.zh: 多位国家元首与政府首脑（法国总统雅克·希拉克、德国总理格哈德·施罗德、英国首相托尼·布莱尔、荷兰首相维姆·科克、比利时首相居伊·费尔霍夫施塔特、卢森堡首相让-克洛德·容克、爱尔兰总理伯蒂·埃亨、奥地利总理沃尔夫冈·许塞尔、瑞士联邦主席阿道夫·奥吉等）
ruler.en: Various heads of state and government (including French President Jacques Chirac, German Chancellor Gerhard Schröder, UK Prime Minister Tony Blair, Dutch Prime Minister Wim Kok, Belgian Prime Minister Guy Verhofstadt, Luxembourg Prime Minister Jean-Claude Juncker, Irish Taoiseach Bertie Ahern, Austrian Chancellor Wolfgang Schüssel, Swiss Federal President Adolf Ogi, and others)

rulerTitle.zh: 总统、首相、国王、总理、联邦主席等
rulerTitle.en: Presidents, prime ministers, kings, chancellors, federal presidents, and others

capital.zh: 巴黎、柏林、伦敦、阿姆斯特丹、布鲁塞尔及其他首都
capital.en: Paris, Berlin, London, Amsterdam, Brussels, and other capitals

governmentForm.zh: 主权民族国家组成的超国家一体化邦联体系，以欧洲联盟、北约、西欧国家间条约网络与共同市场机制为核心
governmentForm.en: A supranational integrating confederational system of sovereign nation-states centered on the European Union, NATO, intergovernmental treaties, and the common market

socialStructure.zh: 高度城市化的后工业社会结构，以中产阶级为主体，辅以专业技术阶层、产业工人、服务业劳动者、金融与商业精英、农民及新移民社群
socialStructure.en: A highly urbanized post-industrial social structure dominated by the middle class, alongside professional and technical strata, industrial workers, service-sector labor, financial and business elites, farmers, and newer immigrant communities

rulingClass.zh: 民选政治精英、国家官僚、欧盟机构官员、跨国企业管理层、金融资本集团及传统王室与社会名流共同构成的复合统治阶层
rulingClass.en: A composite ruling class of elected political elites, state bureaucracies, EU officials, multinational corporate managers, financial capital groups, and residual royal and social notables

succession.zh: 各成员国依其宪法实行选举继任、议会组阁或君主世袭；超国家层面通过条约、理事会协商、议会任命与成员国轮值机制运作
succession.en: Succession occurs through constitutional elections, parliamentary formation, or hereditary monarchy depending on the member state; the supranational level operates through treaties, council negotiation, parliamentary appointment, and rotating mechanisms among member states

structure.zh: 政治结构分为国家层级与超国家层级。国家层级由议会、内阁、总统或君主及司法机关组成；超国家层级以欧洲理事会、欧盟理事会、欧盟委员会、欧洲议会、欧洲法院、欧洲中央银行及相关部长理事会为枢纽。英国、瑞士等在2000年与欧元区或欧盟整合程度不一，但整体仍属于西欧一体化核心圈
structure.en: The political structure is divided between national and supranational layers. The national layer consists of parliaments, cabinets, presidents or monarchs, and judicial systems; the supranational layer centers on the European Council, Council of the European Union, European Commission, European Parliament, European Court of Justice, European Central Bank, and related ministerial councils. The UK and Switzerland differed in their degree of integration with the euro area or the EU in 2000, yet the region as a whole belonged to the core circle of Western European integration

name.zh: 欧洲理事会与成员国首脑会议体系

function.zh: 确定区域总体战略方向，协调外交、安全、扩员、条约改革与重大经济议题
function.en: Sets overall regional strategic direction and coordinates foreign policy, security, enlargement, treaty reform, and major economic issues

function.zh: 提出立法草案，执行共同政策，监管竞争、农业、贸易、区域发展、环境和预算执行
function.en: Drafts legislation and administers common policies, overseeing competition, agriculture, trade, regional development, environment, and budget implementation

function.zh: 审议法案、监督委员会、参与预算程序并代表跨国民意
function.en: Debates legislation, supervises the Commission, participates in the budget process, and represents transnational public opinion

name.zh: 欧洲中央银行与各国中央银行协调体系
name.en: European Central Bank and Central Banking Coordination System

function.zh: 维护欧元区货币稳定，制定利率政策，管理通胀预期与支付清算框架
function.en: Maintains monetary stability for the euro area, sets interest-rate policy, and manages inflation expectations and payments-clearing frameworks

name.zh: 各成员国财政、内政、国防、司法与外交部门
name.en: National Ministries of Finance, Interior, Defense, Justice, and Foreign Affairs

function.zh: 负责税收、治安、社会福利、国防、司法行政、外交谈判及国内公共服务
function.en: Handle taxation, public order, welfare, defense, judicial administration, diplomatic negotiation, and domestic public services

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政高度制度化，通常分为大区、省、州、郡、县、市镇等多层结构。法国偏重中央集权行政区，德国、奥地利、瑞士则具有较强联邦或州级自治，英国、荷兰、比利时、爱尔兰、卢森堡各有地方议会、市政与省级管理体系
localAdmin.en: Local administration was highly institutionalized, commonly divided into regions, provinces, Länder, counties, departments, municipalities, and communes. France leaned toward centralized territorial administration, while Germany, Austria, and Switzerland had stronger federal or cantonal autonomy; the UK, Netherlands, Belgium, Ireland, and Luxembourg each maintained local parliamentary, municipal, and provincial systems

legalSystem.zh: 以大陆法系为主，英国与爱尔兰采用普通法传统，瑞士、荷兰、比利时、德国、法国、奥地利与卢森堡具有成文法与宪政司法框架，并受欧洲人权法与欧盟法逐步深度影响
legalSystem.en: Civil-law systems predominated, while the UK and Ireland followed common-law traditions. Switzerland, the Netherlands, Belgium, Germany, France, Austria, and Luxembourg possessed codified constitutional judicial frameworks and were increasingly shaped by European human-rights law and EU law

taxationSystem.zh: 税制成熟且负担较高，以个人所得税、企业税、增值税、工资税、社会保险缴费、关税与消费税为主体，用于支持福利国家、基础设施、教育、医疗与共同体预算
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mature and relatively heavy, centered on personal income taxes, corporate taxes, value-added tax, payroll taxes, social insurance contributions, customs, and excise taxes, supporting welfare states, infrastructure, education, healthcare, and community budgets

religion.zh: 基督教传统仍为文化根基，其中天主教、新教与英国国教影响深远，但社会整体快速世俗化；同时存在犹太教、伊斯兰教及其他宗教少数群体
religion.en: Christian tradition remained the cultural foundation, with Catholicism, Protestantism, and Anglicanism exerting deep influence, though society was rapidly secularizing; Jewish, Muslim, and other religious minorities were also present

philosophy.zh: 以启蒙理性、自由民主、社会市场经济、社会民主、基督教民主、人权法治、多元文化主义与欧洲一体化理念为主导
philosophy.en: Dominant philosophies included Enlightenment rationalism, liberal democracy, the social market economy, social democracy, Christian democracy, human rights, rule of law, multiculturalism, and European integration

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母，各国语言拥有标准化正字法和现代印刷、广播、数字传播体系
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was dominant, with national languages possessing standardized orthographies and modern print, broadcast, and digital transmission systems

culturalAchievements.zh: 该地区在文学、古典音乐、歌剧、建筑、哲学、电影、视觉艺术、设计、时尚、现代科学与高等教育方面具有世界性影响力。到2000年，西欧已形成从巴黎、伦敦、柏林、阿姆斯特丹、维也纳到布鲁塞尔和苏黎世的密集文化网络，博物馆、大学、出版社、公共电视与艺术节极其发达
culturalAchievements.en: The region exercised global influence in literature, classical music, opera, architecture, philosophy, film, visual art, design, fashion, modern science, and higher education. By 2000, Western Europe had formed a dense cultural network stretching from Paris, London, Berlin, and Amsterdam to Vienna, Brussels, and Zurich, with highly developed museums, universities, publishing houses, public broadcasting, and festivals

languageFamily.zh: 以印欧语系为主，包括罗曼语族、日耳曼语族与凯尔特语族；另有少数民族语言与广泛使用的英语、法语、德语作为国际和区域交流语言
languageFamily.en: The Indo-European family predominated, including Romance, Germanic, and Celtic branches; minority languages also survived, while English, French, and German served widely as international and regional languages

gdpDescription.zh: 2000年前后的西欧与欧盟核心区是世界最富裕、最成熟的经济区域之一，拥有高生产率工业、全球领先的金融与保险业、庞大服务业、发达的基础设施和高度整合的区域市场。欧元于1999年以记账货币形式启动，标志货币一体化进入新阶段
gdpDescription.en: Around 2000, Western Europe and the core EU zone formed one of the richest and most mature economic regions in the world, with high-productivity industry, globally leading finance and insurance, a vast service sector, advanced infrastructure, and a deeply integrated regional market. The euro had been launched in 1999 as a book currency, marking a new phase of monetary integration

mainIndustries.zh: 汽车制造、机械工程、航空航天、化工与制药、金融保险、银行业、航运物流、电子与精密仪器、食品加工、能源、公用事业、零售贸易、旅游、高等教育与创意产业
mainIndustries.en: Automobile manufacturing, mechanical engineering, aerospace, chemicals and pharmaceuticals, finance and insurance, banking, shipping and logistics, electronics and precision instruments, food processing, energy, utilities, retail trade, tourism, higher education, and creative industries

tradeGoods.zh: 汽车、工业机械、化工品、药品、飞机及零部件、金融服务、奢侈品、食品饮料、电子设备、精密仪器、钢铁、塑料制品及专业服务
tradeGoods.en: Automobiles, industrial machinery, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, aircraft and parts, financial services, luxury goods, food and beverages, electronics, precision instruments, steel products, plastics, and professional services

name.zh: 欧元、英镑、瑞士法郎及其他国家货币
name.en: Euro, pound sterling, Swiss franc, and other national currencies

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富总体较高，住房、养老金、储蓄、保险和金融资产广泛普及，但各国之间以及地区内部存在财富分布差异，伦敦、巴黎、苏黎世、卢森堡等金融中心财富密度尤其突出
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was generally high, with widespread homeownership, pensions, savings, insurance, and financial assets, though significant disparities existed between countries and within regions; wealth concentration was especially pronounced in financial centers such as London, Paris, Zurich, and Luxembourg

unit.zh: 国际元（年进出口总额，约当年美元）
unit.en: international dollars (annual total trade, approx. current-year USD)

economicSystem.zh: 以资本主义市场经济为基础，结合强福利国家、社会伙伴协商、工会参与、中央银行独立、跨境单一市场和部分货币联盟的混合制度
economicSystem.en: A mixed system based on capitalist market economies combined with strong welfare states, social partnership bargaining, union participation, independent central banking, a cross-border single market, and partial monetary union

source.zh: 关税、财产税及其他收入
source.en: Customs, property taxes, and other revenue

category.zh: 社会保障、养老金与福利
基础设施、区域发展与农业支持
category.en: Infrastructure, regional development, and agricultural support

category.zh: 行政、债务利息及其他支出
category.en: Administration, debt interest, and other spending

unit.zh: 国际元（公共可支配财政储备与流动资金估计）
unit.en: international dollars (estimated public liquid reserves and treasury resources)

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政能力强，信用等级高，可依赖成熟的国债市场、中央银行体系和深厚金融市场进行再融资；但不同国家债务水平差异明显，福利承诺与人口老龄化构成长期压力
